example_profile_summary: 35, retirement in ~30 years, 10k lump sum + 500/mo, risk 7/10

recommendation.explanation: To help you reach your retirement goal over the next 30 years, we have designed a diversified portfolio consisting entirely of equity ETFs. This portfolio invests in the iShares Core S&P 500 ETF [1], the Vanguard Developed Markets ETF [2], and the iShares MSCI World ETF [3]. These funds focus on company profits and historically offer the highest long-term returns, though their value can fluctuate in the short term [1, 2, 3]. To get started, you may consider an initial investment of 10,000 EUR supplemented by a monthly savings plan of 500 EUR. All selected funds have costs deducted automatically from their assets, allowing more of the market return to stay with you [1, 2, 3]. If you would like a different balance of risk and potential return, please let us know so we can adjust the portfolio for you.

sources.text:
# iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV)

Data as of 2026-07-08. Sources: fund metadata from the project universe, live fundamentals and 5-year price history via Yahoo Finance.

## What this fund is

This is an equity (stock) ETF. Equity funds participate in company profits and historically offer the highest long-term returns, but their value can fluctuate strongly in the short term.

iShares Core S&P 500 ETF trades under the ticker IVV in the category 'Large Blend'.
Fund size: 888.1 billion USD.

## Costs

The total expense ratio (TER) is 0.03% per year. Costs are deducted from the fund's assets automatically; lower cost means more of the market return stays with the investor.

## Risk profile (computed from 5-year price history)

- Annualized volatility: 17.0%
- Annualized return: 13.3%
- Maximum drawdown: -24.5%
- Sharpe ratio: 0.82

## What this fund is

This is an equity (stock) ETF. Equity funds participate in company profits and historically offer the highest long-term returns, but their value can fluctuate strongly in the short term.

Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets Index Fund ETF Shares trades under the ticker VEA in the category 'Foreign Large Blend'.
Fund size: 316.3 billion USD.

## Costs

The total expense ratio (TER) is 0.03% per year. Costs are deducted from the fund's assets automatically; lower cost means more of the market return stays with the investor.

## Risk profile (computed from 5-year price history)
